Risk Description: Because of repeated defect reopenings (reopen count 0-2 across sprints) and incomplete fixes requiring 2-3 retesting cycles per defect, there is a risk that QA budget overruns continue (₹25,000 over budget), which could lead to further financial loss and delayed delivery milestones.

Because of recurring environment access delays (UAT/prod-like environments), PR approval delays (architecture reviewer), and blocked tasks (access permissions, certificate renewal), there is a risk that critical path activities are delayed by 5-6 days per cycle, which could lead to SLA breach and delayed go-live.

Because of consistent PR approval delays (1-2 days per PR across all sprints) due to architecture reviewer dependency, there is a risk that bug fix closure is delayed, which could lead to increased cycle time and defect leakage into production.

Because of increased production monitoring activities (log analysis, exception alerts, retry behavior) and environment dependencies, there is a risk that DevOps budget overruns continue (₹20,000 over budget), which could lead to further financial strain and resource reallocation.

Because of medium defect leakage (3-4%) and reopen counts (0-2) across sprints 4-8, there is a risk that defects escape to production, which could lead to customer dissatisfaction, SLA breach, and reputational damage.
Potential Impact: Customer dissatisfaction, SLA breach, reputational damage

Risk Description: Because of client UAT signoff delays and defect retest queue blocking (blocked status in DSR), there is a risk that UAT closure is delayed, which could lead to delayed go-live and contractual penalties.

Because of cutover checklist approval delays and interface freeze confirmation blocking (DSR remarks), there is a risk that production cutover is delayed, which could lead to go-live postponement and business disruption.

Because of higher developer workload (82% vs 70% planned) due to bug fixes and PR delays, and increased QA utilization (24% vs 20% planned) due to retesting cycles, there is a risk that team burnout occurs, which could lead to attrition and quality degradation.

Because of multiple sick leave instances (Nisha on 04-Mar, Priya M on 09-Apr, Divya on 28-Mar) with backup coverage dependencies, there is a risk that backup owners are overburdened, which could lead to task delays and quality issues.

Because of Bala's roll-off after hypercare handover (Sprint 8 remarks) and transition of support responsibilities, there is a risk that knowledge loss occurs, which could lead to production support gaps and incident resolution delays.
Potential Impact: Production support gaps, incident resolution delays
